clinical_coherence.notes: 31F South Asian with PCOS, family autoimmune history (SLE in maternal aunt), presenting with classic NMDAR encephalitis phenotype. Clinical picture is coherent. However, the PMH template (PCOS, asthma) and family history (maternal aunt with SLE/autoimmune) is very similar to S01 and other NMDAR cases, suggesting template reuse.

tool_output_consistency.notes: CSF shows lymphocytic pleocytosis (72 cells), elevated protein (65.8), normal glucose (66.5), positive NMDAR antibodies at 1:256. EEG with extreme delta brush. MRI with bilateral temporal FLAIR changes. All outputs concordant.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G04.81 correct. Critical actions are clinically appropriate. Ovarian teratoma identified on follow-up imaging as expected.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 31F South Asian with PCOS and family autoimmune history is realistic. Autoimmune predisposition is well-documented as a risk factor.

educational_value.notes: Good individual case but substantial overlap with NMDAR-ENC-S01. Similar demographic (young woman), same clinical phenotype, same EEG findings, same CSF profile. Limited incremental educational value within the set.

issues_found: Template similarity with S01 and other NMDAR straightforward cases: same PMH pattern (PCOS, asthma), same family history pattern (maternal aunt with autoimmune disease), very similar CSF profiles
Limited incremental educational value compared to S01
strengths: Left-handed patient is a rare and realistic detail
PCOS and family autoimmune history provide predisposition context
